title: Conchidium jerdonianum
intro: Conchidium jerdonianum, also known as the Indian orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. The Indian orchid has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Conchidium jerdonianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the Indian orchid is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs like ghee and honey, and used to treat various health conditions.
